title: carex caucasica subsp. perfusca
intro: Carex caucasica subsp. perfusca, also known as Caucasian sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family, characterized by its dark green, linear leaves and drooping, brownish-green flower spikes. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Carex caucasica subsp. perfusca include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a remedy for digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
